sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a green leaf, a leaf newly plucked, so called from tearing off (compare Gen 8:11). Eze 17:9. (Ch. טַרְפָא, Syr. ܛܰܪܦܳܐ id.)

(2) an animal torn in pieces, the prey of a wild beast. Job 4:11 29:17 38:39 . הַרְרֵי טֶרֶף “mountains of prey,” i.e. of depredators, thieves, whence they come forth for booty, Psa 76:5.

(3) food, Pro 31:15 Mal 3:10 Psal. 111:5 comp. the verb in Hiphil.
